SURYA ROSHNI Q4 : Management says Looking ahead to FY27, we are targeting overall volumes of approximately 11 lakh tonnes representing a growth of approximately 21-22% over FY26 volumes supported by improving utilisation levels, phased commissioning of new capacities and stronger contribution from value-added products. 

On the export front, we are targeting to cross 2.5 lakh tonnes during FY27, a significant step-up from 1.41 lakh tonnes achieved in FY26. 

With rising infrastructure investments, expansion in oil & gas pipeline networks, industrial capex and increasing export opportunities, we remain confident about the long- term growth outlook for the Steel Pipes & Strips business.

Here’s why GAIL jumped 5% despite mixed Q4 & FY26 results 👇

Q4 FY26 Highlights
• Revenue from Operations: ₹35,705 Cr (↓ 2.3% YoY | ↑ 1.1% QoQ)
• PAT: ₹1,481 Cr (↓ 40.9% YoY | ↓ 14.3% QoQ)

FY26 Highlights
• Revenue from Operations: ₹1,42,094 Cr (↓ 0.2% YoY)
• EBITDA: ₹14,524 Cr (↓ 30% YoY)
• PAT: ₹7,581 Cr (↓ 42% YoY)
• Debt-to-Equity Ratio: 0.28x

Operational Metrics
• Natural Gas Transmission: 122.18 MMSCMD (↓ 3.8% YoY)
• Natural Gas Marketing Volume: 104.21 MMSCMD (↑ 3.2% YoY)
• LPG Transmission: 4.58 MMTPA (↑ 2.7% YoY)- Highest Ever
• Petrochemical Sales: 785 TMT (↓ 7.1% YoY)

Key Business Updates
• 18,000+ km gas pipeline network with 65% market share
• LNG portfolio at 16.56 MMTPA
• Plans to source an additional 4–5 MMTPA LNG by 2030
• Renewable portfolio expansion from 155 MW to 1,063 MW in progress

🔍 Analysis
Despite pressure on profitability due to weaker gas margins and global energy volatility, GAIL’s core transmission and marketing businesses remained resilient. The company continues to strengthen India’s gas infrastructure with pipeline expansion, LNG sourcing, petrochemical capacity additions, and renewable energy investments. Operationally, gas marketing volumes and LPG transmission remained strong, showing healthy domestic demand trends. The long-term India gasification theme, LNG expansion, and clean-energy transition continue to remain key structural growth drivers for GAIL. The optimism in the stock came as crude oil prices declined amid reports of progress in US-Iran negotiations, which have revived hopes of the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

Consumer Preference

Premium Delivery Opportunity

- 70% Indian businesses see premium delivery willingness from consumers

Industry Insight
- Report suggests logistics sector may shift focus from speed to reliability and execution efficiency

Park Medi World accelerates growth with acquisition of Medicity Hospital, Rudrapur.

Park Medi World Limited has announced acquisition of The Medicity Hospital in Rudrapur, Uttarakhand, marking a significant step in its expansion journey. Through this transaction, the company is acquiring V3 Healthcare Private Limited, which owns and operates the hospital, in a deal valued at approximately ₹177 Cr.

The Medicity Hospital is a well-established multi-super speciality facility with a capacity of around 330 beds and is one of the largest healthcare institutions in the Kumaon region. It offers a wide range of advanced medical services across more than 20 specialties and is supported by modern infrastructure and a strong clinical team.

This acquisition marks Park Medi’s entry into Uttarakhand and strengthens its presence across North India. The move aligns with the company’s strategy of expanding into high-potential regions and building a wider, integrated healthcare network. By adding a large and established hospital to its portfolio, the company aims to enhance service offerings, improve operational efficiency, and drive long-term growth.

The transaction will be completed in phases, with a majority stake to be acquired in the near term and the remaining stake over the long term. Overall, this acquisition is expected to strengthen the company’s footprint, improve its scale, and support sustained value creation going forward.

IRFC SIGNS ₹13,527 CR HYDERABAD METRO REFINANCING DEAL 🚆💰

• 🤝 IRFC signed ₹13,527 Cr refinancing agreement with L&T Metro Rail (Hyderabad)

• 🚇 Deal aimed at refinancing Hyderabad Metro Rail debt obligations

• 🏙️ Marks one of India’s landmark urban rail financing transactions

• 🇮🇳 Supports Government’s urban mobility & Viksit Bharat vision

• 📍 Hyderabad Metro Phase-1 spans 69.2 km across 3 corridors with 57 stations

• 👥 Metro network currently serves over 5 lakh daily passengers

• 💵 Refinancing to replace higher-cost debt with competitively priced long-term funding

• 📆 Structured over 20-year tenure with quarterly repayments

• 🛡️ Backed by Telangana Government undertaking, guarantee & RBI-backed debit mandate

• 🚄 IRFC sees this as a major step in diversifying beyond traditional railway financing

🟢 Impact: Positive — Large refinancing deal strengthens IRFC’s infrastructure financing portfolio and boosts visibility in urban mobility funding opportunities.

Anondita Medicare’s H2FY26 numbers show one clear theme: scale + margins improving together.

Revenue jumped from ₹45.59 Cr to ₹83.32 Cr — up 82.77% YoY.

But the stronger part is profitability:

EBITDA: ₹15.93 Cr → ₹31.46 Cr
PAT: ₹10.47 Cr → ₹21.28 Cr
EBITDA Margin: 34.94% → 37.76%
At the same time, the company is preparing for its next leg of growth with the Noida facility expansion.
Key expansion details:
• Approved capacity addition: ~1,360 million pieces p.a.
• Existing capacity: 869 million pieces p.a.
• 16 manufacturing lines under WIP
• Estimated project cost: ~₹75 Cr
• Promoters infused ₹30 Cr via preferential issue at ₹1,022/share

**Key Growth Drivers**

1. Global Expansion Scale exports beyond Africa into high-potential international markets

2. In advanced stages, enabling access to global markets, with exports targeted to begin by end-FY26 or early next Q1FY26

3. Enhance quality, scalability, and margins through advanced manufacturing

4. Launch patented female condoms to address underserved demand

The company has signed MoUs with Chalukya Power and Pickrenew Energy, adding 110 MWh of Battery Energy Storage System opportunities for FY26-27.

The company is now targeting ₹200 Cr orderbook in the Battery & BESS segment within FY26-27.

• Battery technology
• System integration
• Project development
• After-sales support

reports.text:
JPM on LTM
Downgrade to N, TP cut to Rs 4500 from Rs 5100

LTM announced that it has issued an offer to acquire Randstad’s technology and consulting services business in Europe and Australia.

Revenues of these entities (€469m) have declined sharply in the past two years while estimate business operates at much lower 4-5% Ebitda margins, given its onshore-heavy nature, which should be materially margin-dilutive.

LTM has also announced concomitant contracts to take over and build a GCC for Randstad in India and a reverse contract to Randstad to support LTM’s subcon needs
While, across three contracts, management doesn’t see any EPS dilution, estimate acquisition alone could drive 2% EPS dilution due to amortization costs and lower interest income.

Moreover, don’t see obvious horizontal service line or vertical synergies, given its focus on digital engineering and cybersecurity rather than core IT Services, which LTM focuses on.
Cross-sell opportunities as they exist should be limited by different buying centers for engineering and IT services

Acquisition price appears to be modest at €160m on a EV/Sales basis (0.34x), but not on implied profit multiples (8x EBITDA, 13-15x EBIT), given global/EU peer multiples.

More than price paid, see this acquisition as a sign that organic growth opportunities are drying up and management is being forced to evaluate unusual acquisitions to bulk up rather than thoughtfully add capabilities. Fear management distraction, due to closure of the acquisition by 3Q27 and ekeing out of synergies after that, could weigh on execution.

Moderate organic revenues/EPS by 1-2%, cut target PE multiple from 21x to 19x

VINDHYA TELELINKS : EPC BUSINESS IMPACTED BY JJM PAYMENT DELAYS 🚰🏗️

• 📉 EPC segment revenue declined to ₹735.28 Cr in Q4 FY26 vs ₹1,033.05 Cr YoY

• 💰 EBITDA fell to ₹49.99 Cr from ₹74.93 Cr in Q4 FY25

• ⏳ Execution impacted by delays in fund releases under key government infra projects, especially UP-JJM

• 🏦 Elevated working capital pressure and slower project monetisation affected performance

• 📉 Receivables reduced sharply to ~₹705.77 Cr as of March 2026 from ~₹956.16 Cr in Dec 2025 due to improved collections during Q4

• 🚰 Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) allocation initially revised down from ~₹67,000 Cr to ~₹17,000 Cr due to audit/procedural delays

• 🔄 Cabinet has now restored JJM allocation to ~₹67,670 Cr after approving extension of the scheme

• 🤝 Company actively engaging with central & state authorities for faster payment clearances

• 📅 Management expects meaningful recovery of outstanding dues by end of CY2026

• ⚙️ Focus remains on execution ramp-up, disciplined cost management & improving cash cycles

- Secured ₹1.23 Cr order from Dental College & Hospital, Gujarat
- Order includes interactive panels, CCTV systems & displays

Product Scope
- Includes multimedia projectors and network infrastructure
- Also covers lecterns, printers and ethernet cables

FABTECH TECHNOLOGIES – NEW ORDER WIN  

Order Details
- Received ₹65.10 Cr solar industry order
- Order awarded by domestic Solar PV Module manufacturer
- Includes modular cleanroom systems and allied accessories

Execution Update
- Project targeted for completion by November 30, 2026

Business Impact
- Strengthens overall order book
- Highlights cleanroom execution capabilities

GOVT SOURCES – FUEL PRICE UPDATE  

Price Revision Update
- OMCs raised fuel prices after 76 days of absorption
- Petrol prices now above ₹112/litre in Andhra Pradesh, Telangana & Kerala

State-Wise Trend
- Highest fuel prices concentrated in several Congress and I.N.D.I.A bloc-ruled states
- Andhra Pradesh among highest VAT-levying states
- Lowest fuel prices seen in Gujarat, UP, Delhi, Haryana, Goa & Assam

Government Commentary
- India highlighted as major economy to cut fuel prices during energy crises
- Most importing nations passed higher fuel costs to consumers
- OMC fuel price hikes among lowest globally

ZAGGLE PREPAID – MANAGEMENT UPDATE  

Key Update
- Chief Technology Officer resigned due to personal reasons
- Last working day will be May 27, 2026

KAMDHENU – PROPERTY DEAL UPDATE  

Transaction Update
- Cancelled proposed property acquisition in Alwar, Rajasthan
- Decision taken following due diligence observations

Management Update
- Board may reconsider transaction later
- Subject to satisfactory resolution of due diligence issues

CONNPLEX CINEMAS – NEW MULTIPLEX LAUNCH  

Expansion Update
- Opened new 4-screen multiplex in Ahmedabad
- Located at Center Point, Adani Shantigram

Facility Highlights
- Total seating capacity of 279 seats
- Equipped with Dolby 7.1 audio systems
- Includes high-performance sound and screen setup

ELGI EQUIPMENTS – CREDIT RATING UPDATE  

Rating Details
- Long-term rating reaffirmed at ‘CRISIL AA/Stable’
- Short-term rating reaffirmed at ‘CRISIL A1+’
- Ratings cover ₹557.4 Cr bank facilities

Key Takeaway
- Reaffirmation reflects strong financial stability
- Indicates healthy credit profile

PTC INDIA – CREDIT RATING UPDATE  

Rating Details
- CRISIL reaffirmed ‘CRISIL A1+’ rating on ₹5,500 Cr bank facilities
- ₹300 Cr commercial paper programme also rated ‘CRISIL A1+’

Rating Significance
- Reflects very strong safety profile

BIGGEST COMEBACK STOCKS OF THE YEAR 🚀📈  

Top Performers
- Sterlite Technologies: +522%
- MTAR Technologies: +472%
- Bajaj Consumer Care: +225%
- Quality Power Electrical: +212%
- Venus Remedies: +211%

Strong Gainers
- Ather Energy: +195%
- GE Power: +193%
- TD Power: +187%
- Acutaas Chemicals: +183%
- GE Vernova T&D: +173%

Other Major Movers
- MCX: +166%
- Lumax Auto Technologies: +160%
- Hindustan Copper: +148%
- Sansera Engineering: +139%
- HFCL: +137%

Key Trend
- Rally led by power, defence, EMS, railways, capital goods and manufacturing themes
- Several stocks more than doubled from yearly lows

BANK OF BARODA – FY27 FISCAL DEFICIT WARNING  

Key Estimates
- West Asia conflict could widen FY27 fiscal deficit to 4.7%-4.8% of GDP
- Estimated fiscal gap may rise to ~₹2.1 lakh Cr

Key Risks
- Higher fertiliser subsidies and possible fuel excise cuts
- OMCs facing estimated daily losses of ~₹750 Cr

Offsetting Factor
- RBI’s record ₹2.86 lakh Cr dividend may partly cushion fiscal pressure

SEBI CRACKS DOWN ON FINFLUENCER-LED STOCK MANIPULATION

Hemant Gupta and 6 others accused in SEBI's ₹20 crore SME stock pump-and-dump investigation

SEBI alleges family-run network bought shares and then used X, Telegram, WhatsApp to promote them publicly

SEBI alleges investors lured using “multibagger”, “upper circuit” style stock messaging

Probe claims accused sold shares after retail investors pushed prices higher

RPP INFRA – NEW WORK ORDER  

Order Details
- Received ₹5.13 Cr SIPCOT work order including GST
- Project located at SIPCOT Industrial Park, Shoolagiri
- Scope includes removal of retrieved hard rock materials

Execution Update
- Project execution timeline of 12 months
- Work to commence within 15 days of order receipt
- Company to deposit ₹25.65 Lakhs as security amount

UKMTO: 

Incident involving an oil tanker near Yemen:

- The UK Maritime Operations Organization today reported receiving information about a security incident involving an oil tanker in waters near the island of Socotra.

IGL hikes CNG prices again 🚨

⏩₹1/kg increase from 6 am, May 23-third hike this month, total up ₹4/kg

⏩Move driven by higher input costs & strong USD, but CNG still up to 45% cheaper vs alternate fuels

Nikkei briefly dipped under 60k as AI and semiconductor stocks crashed. JPM says it’s a technical overheating issue, not a fundamental economic crisis. A short-term cooling off.

JPM’s Z-score model shows momentum surged to +3.90, then crashed to -5.30. Expect momentum to stay dead for 1 month. Smart move? Rotate money into classic Value stocks.

Regional banks are the ultimate hedge right now. They mix cheap Value with steady Momentum. Plus, they win big as the BOJ stays hawkish and hikes interest rates from June.

🎯 JPM Summary: Don't panic dump. Tech is just cooling down. Lock your capital into Japanese regional banks. They offer zero pain trades and clear profits from rising interest rates.

CNG has become more expensive in New Delhi following a Rs 1 price hike reportedly issued by Indraprastha Gas Ltd. 

This marks the third time CNG prices have been raised in the last 10 days. 

The government had raised CNG prices by Rs 2 on May 15 and then again by Re 1 on May 18.

GLENMARK PHARMACEUTICALS – U.S. LITIGATION SETTLEMENT  

Settlement Details
- Glenmark USA to pay $29.63 Million over 5 years
- Settlement related to U.S. antitrust litigation
- Agreement subject to court approval

Company Statement
- Company denied allegations and liability
- Financial impact expected to be limited due to existing provisions

WINDSOR MACHINES – LAND ACQUISITION UPDATE  

Acquisition Details
- Acquired ~77,198 sq. meters land in Rajkot, Gujarat
- Transaction value stands at ₹55 Cr
- Ownership transfer completed on May 22, 2026

Business Impact
- Land acquired for future industrial expansion
- Supports long-term business growth plans

After MTAR, Sterlite Tech promoters have started dumping...
But, stock is still stuck in upper cicuit.

Ideaforge 

Drone gets DGCA Certification allowing commercial deployment across India for survey, mapping, aerial surveillance and infra inspection.

More Civil use case for Ideaforge to diversify from defence.

LINDE INDIA – STAKE DILUTION UPDATE  

Transaction Details
- Stake in Zenataris Renewable Energy reduced to 19.18% from 27%
- Dilution resulted from fresh equity allotment by Zenataris

Key Update
- Change due to equity dilution and not stake sale by Linde India
- Investment linked to renewable energy initiatives

NSE To Remove Exide Industries And Nuvama Wealth From F&O Segment Effective July 29 2026

Existing May, June And July 2026 Contracts To Continue Till Expiry; New Strikes Will Still Be Introduced In Current Contract Months

KEVIN WARSH, FED CHAIR SAYS

I Will Lead A Reform-Oriented Fed

With Independence And Resolve, Inflation Can Be Lower

Coming Years Can Bring Unmatched Prosperity

Honor Of A Lifetime To Re-enter Public Service

Entities Identified where Hemant Gupta operated as per SEBI Order

Strictly not to consider the CG issue against the company 

Might be promoters were involved in some cases but not all

While the document contains a complete list of all 82 scrips, it specifically highlights a few as detailed case studies to illustrate the operators' coordinated modus operandi of accumulation, hype, and liquidation:

*   **Afcom Holdings Limited**
*   **K2 Infragen Limited**
*   **Sona Machinery Limited**

Other scrips frequently referenced as examples of their social media manipulation, X (Twitter) posts, and private WhatsApp/Telegram chat instructions include **Kalyani Cast-Tech Ltd**, **Kay Cee Energy & Infra Ltd**, **Influx Healthtech Ltd**, and **Freshara Agro Exports Ltd**. In their private Telegram chats, the operators also discussed target prices for other companies and peers, including **Teerth Gopicon**, **ATMASTCO**, **Z-Tech (India) Ltd**, and unlisted entities like **AITMC Venture**.

FORTIS HEALTHCARE – Q4 HIGHLIGHTS  

Hospital Business
- Hospital revenue rose 19% YoY to ₹2,023 Cr
- EBITDA increased 19.9% YoY to ₹446 Cr
- Occupied beds grew 17% YoY

Operational Highlights
- ARPOB improved to ₹2.56 Cr p.a.
- Radiation Therapy & Robotic Surgery volumes grew strongly
- International patient revenue increased 18.5% YoY in FY26

Expansion Update
- Added ~500 beds through acquisitions & leases
- Acquired People Tree Hospital and Shrimann Hospital
- Diagnostics network expanded to 4,445 touch points

Diagnostics & Preventive Care
- Diagnostics revenue grew 11.1% YoY to ₹387 Cr
- Diagnostics EBITDA rose 35.9% YoY to ₹85 Cr
- Preventive healthcare revenue grew 21% YoY in FY26

DAM CAPITAL – MANAGEMENT COMMENTARY  

Business Update
- Merchant Banking revenue impacted by delayed IPO launches
- Geopolitical uncertainties affected deal timelines
- Broking revenue moderated amid volatile markets

Operational Update
- Broking business continued to provide stability
- Employee count increased to 130 from 125 YoY
- Rental expenses increased due to new office premise

IRFC – ECB FUNDRAISING UPDATE  

Fundraising Details

Hohn’s High-Conviction Alpha Trap

Sir Chris Hohn runs a hyper-concentrated $21B book with roughly $14B parked in a single bet on GE Aerospace, weaponizing a multi-decade moat where zero new engine competitors have entered in 50 years.

Retail loves the "charitable billionaire" narrative, but Hohn’s extreme concentration introduces massive systemic tail risk. A single operational drawdown or aerospace de-rating would completely vaporize the fund's capital.

This regime shift highlights a structural corporate margin play: dominant global duopolies can easily pass through inflation-fueled input costs, squeezing labor stats while capturing immense, unchecked pricing power.

Bottom Line: Forget diversification. True alpha in this era is about finding structural monopolies with bulletproof unit economics that can survive any macro liquidity trap.

Interesting Chart - Capex Required to Integrate in Solar Chain goes up significanlty

Waaree’s biggest advantage in this solar cycle may not just be demand.

It is the CAPEX barrier created by deeper backward integration.

The numbers explain it clearly:

Module-only capacity needs ~₹100–150 Cr/GW.
Cell + module integration needs ~₹500–600 Cr/GW.
Ingot + wafer + cell + module needs ~₹1,100–1,200 Cr/GW.
Full poly-to-module integration needs ~₹1,500–1,600 Cr/GW.

That is nearly a 10–17x jump in capital intensity as the value chain goes deeper.
This changes the competitive landscape.
On the supply side, fewer players can afford this scale, capital, technology, and execution capability.
On the demand side, ALMM and domestic sourcing requirements can further shift opportunities toward integrated domestic players.

So Waaree gets a dual-cycle benefit:

Competition reduces as integration becomes capital-heavy

Demand expands for approved, integrated domestic manufacturers

This is why Waaree is not just participating in the solar upcycle.

It is structurally positioned to remain one of the front-runners.

So what everybody sees as an oversupply or competitive issue may not be true

**Nomura on Sansera Engineering**  

**Target Price: ₹3,114 (from ₹2,342)**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

• Diversification strategy gaining strong momentum  
• Q4FY26 performance beat estimates  
• Non-auto business traction remains strong  
• ADS division driving global growth  
• FY27 revenue growth seen ~22%  
• FY28 revenue growth seen ~19%  
• FY27 EBITDA margin seen ~19%  
• FY28 EBITDA margin seen ~19.5%  
• FY27 EPS estimates raised ~8%  
• FY28 EPS estimates raised ~11%  
• Valuation rerating potential remains strong  
• Non-auto diversification improving visibility

Kalyan Capitals Ltd (KCL) – A Quiet Turnaround Story? 

What does the company do?

KCL is registered as a Non-Systemically Important Non-Deposit Taking Base Layer NBFC-ICC.

The company primarily provides funded facilities to individuals and corporates. Now, it has also entered the gold loan business — a segment seeing strong traction across India due to rising gold prices and demand for secured retail lending.

Business Momentum Improving
FY25 loan disbursement stood at ₹203.2 Cr, up ~24% YoY
Net NPA ratio remained around 3%

Valuation Looks Interesting Despite improvement in business activity:

Market Cap: ₹48.4 Cr
Enterprise Value: ₹304 Cr
P/B Ratio: 0.92x
Stock P/E: 12.6x
EV/EBITDA: 10.2x
Current Price: ₹9.21
Book Value: ₹10

The stock currently trades below book value and at a valuation lower than many listed NBFC peers.

Growth Metrics
5-Year Revenue CAGR: 39%
3-Year Revenue CAGR: 40%
TTM Growth: 25%

That kind of growth with improving operations often becomes the start of a turnaround rerating story.

O-P TP Rs 338
reported 28% revenue growth in 4QFY26 and its highest-ever Ebitda margin of 8.4%, up 195bps YoY, with Ebitda 8% above consensus. Beauty NSV grew 29% YoY with margin-accretive own brands up 54%, driving a +70bps Ebitda margin. Fashion saw 42% YoY NSV growth with Ebitda breakeven. Ebit missed our estimate due to higher-than-expected depreciation. We lower our FY27-28CL earnings estimates by 1%-2% on account of higher depreciation, but raise our target price from Rs328 to Rs338 to reflect faster sales growth. We expect Ebitda margin expansion to continue, driven by rising salience of own brands and operating leverage.

Medium-term outlook is quite positive, although mgmt. sounded a note of caution in context of a tough macro.

Hold, TP Rs 273

*CLSA on ITC*
O-P, TP Rs 394
Cigarettes Ebit grew 7.2% YoY in 4QFY26 and was 15% ahead of consensus. The quarter was marked by a significant increase in cigarette taxes, which likely led to channel filling and potential product mix changes due to pricing differentials, thereby impacting overall performance during the period. Cigarettes net revenue for the year grew 8.2% YoY. FMCG revenue other than cigarettes grew 15% YoY and was 2%/5% above our estimate/consensus.

*CITI on ITC*
Sell, TP Rs 290
4Q performance is not directly comparable with prior periods given mid-quarter changes in cigarette taxation
Estimate ~7% volume decline, driving the 24% drop in cigarette net revenues, reflecting: 1) strong January demand; 2) trade inventory stocking ahead of price hikes; and 3) demand slowdown thereafter following staggered price increases to offset higher taxes.
Reported cigarette EBIT growth of ~7% is also not comparable, as it appears supported by one-off inventory gains on pre-tax revision stock
Importantly, key structural uncertainties persist, particularly around price elasticity, demand sensitivity to successive price hikes, and potential downtrading to illicit products

Hold, TP Rs 350

DAM Cap on PI Ind
Sell. TP Rs 2556

FY26 performance underscores structural challenges in Agchem CSM

Legacy products (65–70% of exports) slowed sharply, with recovery back-ended to FY27, while new products remain steady but not sizeable enough to offset pressure on legacy products.

 Domestic agchem pressured by elevated channel inventories, pricing constraints, lower acreages, adverse weather, and regulatory transitions in Biologicals.

PI’s solid balance sheet and execution track record underpin its competitiveness, but growth remains uncertain due to delayed CSM exports recovery, Biologicals revival, and margin pressures from input costs and R&D/NCE launch spend.

EBITDA expansion is constrained despite stable gross margins.
Cut FY27E/FY28E EPS by 15%/16% to factor in higher opex, muted GM improvement, input cost pressures, and back-ended legacy recovery

CITI on PI Ind
Sell, TP Rs 2600

At Rs3.37bn (below), PI’s 4Q EBITDA fell 26% yoy on lower CSM exports and domestic agchem revenues and lower EBITDA margin.

Pharma CDMO revenues grew 23% yoy (accounted for 7% of revenues in 4QFY26), although the segment continued to report EBITDA loss.

EBITDA was up 11% qoq, largely on seasonality.

EBITDA margin was 21.5% vs 22.0% in 3Q and 25.5% in 4QFY25.

Looking ahead while mgmt. expects revenue growth in FY27 – this is likely to be backended given relatively higher base in 1H.

With generic competition for PI’s largest molecule (pyroxasulfone) expected in US in CY26, muted outlook for the global agchem market & no immediate ramp-up of new segments

CLSA on Samvardhana Mortherson
O-P, TP Rs 173

CITI on Ola
Sell, TP Rs 26

Ola’s market share is expected to improve as co focuses on enhancing its service offering.

While acknowledge Ola’s gross margin improvement, we’d look for sustained volume growth before getting more constructive
90% of Ola’s opex is fixed, & if volumes do not improve, EBITDA would be under pressure despite high gross margins

**BPCL – JEFFERIES VIEW**

- EBITDA beats estimates strongly

- Petrol/diesel marketing margins negative
- Recent fuel price hikes insufficient

- Net debt likely to increase
- Valuation attractive versus peers
- Trades at 1.1x forward P/B

- Capex intensity expected to stay high

- Trades at 5.9x FY28F EV/EBITDA

India Power Sector - key insights

India’s total installed capacity reached 533 GW in FY26

FY26 total capacity addition at 58 GW

Renewable generation grew 20%

Installed capacity mix
Thermal capacity at 249 GW
Solar capacity at 150 GW
Wind capacity at 56 GW
Hydro capacity at 51 GW
Nuclear capacity at 9 GW

Solar capacity has increased from 22 GW in FY18 to 150 GW in FY26

That is almost 7x growth in 8 years
FY30E at 288 GW

Cell manufacturing capacity is expected to rise from 25 GW in FY25 to 115 GW by FY30E

Ingot-wafer capacity is expected to rise from 2 GW in FY25 to 80 GW by FY30E

Polysilicon capacity is also expected to start scaling from FY27 onwards

Earlier, the opportunity was mainly in module assembly

Now the opportunity is expanding into cells, wafers, ingots & polysilicon
